name: Type 2 Diabetes - Comprehensive Provider Query (All Paths)
description: Simulates an experienced healthcare provider who knows that not all T2DM patients have proper diagnosis codes in the EMR. The prompt explicitly asks the LLM to search across multiple evidence types (diagnoses, medications, labs) to find the complete T2DM population. This is the ultimate test of LLM clinical informatics capability â€” it requires generating multiple FHIR queries and understanding the clinical reasoning behind each approach.
prompt:
I need to identify my complete type 2 diabetes patient population for a quality improvement initiative. I know from experience that not all diabetic patients have a formal diagnosis code in their chart â€” some were started on metformin or other oral hypoglycemics by outside providers, and others have consistently elevated A1c results but the diagnosis was never formally documented. Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:

1. Patients with a type 2 diabetes diagnosis on their problem list
2. Patients who have been prescribed oral diabetes medications (metformin, sulfonylureas like glipizide or glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors like sitagliptin, or GLP-1 agonists)
3. Patients with hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5%

For each query, return the matching resources. I want to see the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Comprehensive multi-query approach that mirrors how a real clinician would identify the full T2DM population. Requires the LLM to generate 3 separate FHIR queries and combine results. Tests clinical reasoning, FHIR query construction, and multi-resource search strategy.

expected_query.notes: This test case expects MULTIPLE queries. The expected_query.url is the minimum (diagnosis only). The full evaluation should union patient IDs from all 3 queries in metadata.expected_queries to get the complete T2DM population.
